_meta.description: AstroPay brand design tokens — source of truth for color and typography
_meta.sync: Manual — update this file whenever Figma variables are published

figma_color_values._note: Figma Plugin API RGB values (0–1 range)

typography.figma_note: Inter uses 'Semi Bold' with a space — not 'SemiBold'

email_overrides._note: Channel-scoped tokens for the email templates only. Paid media + in-app continue reading from `color`, `color_roles`, `figma_color_values`, and `typography` above. See BRAND.md § 6.1 and figma/components.json -> email_templates.chassis.tokens_used.

gunmetal.role: primary text (H1 + body) on light email background

teal_2025_over_light.role: foreground variant of teal_2025 tuned for use as TEXT or HIGHLIGHT on light surfaces. Used by phrase highlights inside hero H1 / body copy. Figma variable: `Teal 2025 > over Light` in AstroTheme collection.

figma_color_values._note: Figma Plugin API RGB values (0-1 range) for the email-scoped tokens.

figma_variables._note: Bind to these Figma variables when applying tokens via Plugin API. Prefer variable binding (setBoundVariableForPaint) over hardcoded hex/RGB so theme switches and library updates propagate.

teal_2025_over_light.use: Phrase highlight color on body and H1 text (light email surfaces)

typography._note: H1 and body in email continue using Matter (SemiBold / Regular). Only the CTA uses DM Sans Bold. DM Sans may not be installed in working files — fallback to Inter Bold if missing.

email_infinite_overrides._note: Product- AND channel-scoped tokens for the Infinite email library only (figma/components.json -> email_templates_infinite, section 1165:20363). Use ONLY when the brief is explicitly tagged Infinite (Product=Infinite, campaign=Infinite GTM AR, subscription_tier=infinite, or copy anchoring on 'La membresía que se paga sola'). For every other email — Core PIX, Core FX, Core Freelance, Core lifecycle, brand awareness — use email_overrides above. Theme is the single most important early decision: pick Light or Dark upfront and use that theme's tokens consistently across menu, hero, blocks, CTA, icons, and footer. Source of truth in BRAND.md § 6.2. Figma reference: figma/components.json -> email_templates_infinite.tokens_used.

color_light_theme._use_when: Default Infinite theme — lifecycle / welcome / activation / educational / ROI explainer sends.

bg_primary.role: primary email background (cream, NOT pure white)

text_primary.role: primary text on Light theme (same hex as Core gunmetal)

cta_bg.role: CTA pill background on Light theme (solid dark)

cta_text.role: CTA pill text color on Light theme

color_dark_theme._use_when: High-impact moments — trial-end, premium activation, anchor brand sends, retention with urgency.

bg_tertiary.role: card / surface accents on Dark theme

text_primary.role: primary text on Dark theme (cream on dark)

cta_bg.role: CTA pill background on Dark theme (solid light)

cta_text.role: CTA pill text color on Dark theme

figma_color_values._note: Figma Plugin API RGB values (0-1 range) for the Infinite-scoped tokens.

typography._note: Infinite emails share Matter family with Core but use different styles for CTA (Matter Bold, NOT DM Sans). DM Sans is reserved for Core email CTAs — using it in an Infinite send is a wrong-channel-token violation.

forbidden_tokens._note: Tokens that MUST NOT appear in an Infinite email. Flagged by @cd and @guardian as wrong-channel-token violations.

teal_2025.reason: Core email CTA background — never in Infinite

teal500.reason: Paid-media / in-app primary — never in any email

dm_sans.reason: Core email CTA font — Infinite uses Matter Bold for CTAs
